hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'anima' is addressed directly — it is the vocative of address
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'anima' would be 'animae' — 'anima' here is vocative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'anima' would also be 'anima', but context shows direct address (vocative)

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'animae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'animae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'animae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Christus' — 'Christi' has the -i genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Christus' would be 'Christo' — 'Christi' ends in -i (genitive)
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Christus' would be 'Christe' — 'Christi' is genitive

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 3rd sg. would be 'sanctificat' — 'sanctifica' is the imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'sanctifices' — 'sanctifica' is the imperative singular
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'sanctificare' — 'sanctifica' is the imperative singular

hints.sacrifico, sacrificare, sacrificavi, sac: These are the principal parts of 'sacrificare' (to sacrifice)
hints.benedico, benedicere, benedixi, benedict: These are the principal parts of 'benedicere' (to bless)
hints.consecro, consecrare, consecravi, consec: These are the principal parts of 'consecrare' (to consecrate)
